sections.paragraph:
αὐθέντης, ου, ὁ (s. αὐθεντέω; Hdt. et al.; on the mng. s. PKretschmer, Glotta 3, 1912, 289–93; FZucker, SBLeipzAk 107, 4, ’62; the adj. is a loanw. in rabb., and the noun is the source of Turk. ‘effendi’; cp. Jos., Bell. 2, 240) master (Eur., Suppl. 442; epitaph PCairZen 532, 15; Sb 6754, 15; PGM 13, 258) τοῦ πύργου Hs 9, 5, 6.—DELG. Sv.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

adj. adj. = adjective

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 150
